exercises.heading: Environment
exercises.description: Person A {v} As much as I do my best to recycle and use less plastic, it’s really difficult when you’re as busy as I am. I’m almost never home and most of the food I eat is pre-made microwave meals that I get from the supermarket. Obviously the packaging is pure plastic and I’m not sure what a solution to this would look like. I tell myself it’s okay by making sure to recycle everything I possibly can and hopefully in the future I can find even more ways. {v} Person B {v} I’ve based my life around living green for years now and I’m always discovering new ways to change my lifestyle so that it is more environmentally friendly. I think that the media needs to do a better job making the things people can do to help clear, but supermarkets need to start following these guidelines as much as possible too. It seems that not enough people have realized the gravity of the situation we are putting our planet in. {v} Person C {v} Just like anyone else, I recycle as much as I can, I try to take the bus and cycle or walk to places when I can. It helps not having a car and living close to my job. But I can’t help but wonder if just one person doing these little things is actually going to make a difference. Most of my friends don’t pay attention to all of this, so would it be a big deal if I stopped caring even the little bit that I do? Seeing a result would make me feel a lot more encouraged. {v} Person D {v} I hear all this talk about saving the planet by recycling and taking public transportation, but I just really can’t be bothered to change my lifestyle for it. Humans have been living this way for years and it seems weird that only recently we’ve started to pay attention to this. I can respect the people who change their lives to save the environment, but personally, I don’t think that I’ll be paying attention to a greener lifestyle in the near future.
exercises.subtitle: Four people give their opinion about an article on different ways to save the environment. Read their comments and answer the questions.
exercises.label: Reading Part 4

questions.title: 1. Who thinks that there isn’t much of a point in changing their habits to help the environment?
2. Who thinks that changes need to be made outside the home as well as in the home to help the environment?
3. Who thinks that people need to become even more aware of the problem?
4. Who thinks that one person doesn’t make a big difference for the environment?
5. Who walks or uses public transportation as a way to help the environment?
6. Who thinks that they could use less plastic, but isn’t sure how?
7. Who says that they don’t have enough time to recycle?

exercises.label: Reading Part 5
exercises.description: Read the text below. Match the headings A-H to the paragraphs 19-25.

answers: H. How to book your rental car in the UK
F. What do I do if I’m in an accident?
questions.title: 19. Various types of vehicles can be rented in the United Kingdom. The choice of vehicle depends {v} on the needs of the renter. A large family would, of course, rent a big car, while an individual {v} might stick with a mini-class vehicle. Of the many different categories of rental vehicles in the {v} UK, some include mini, economy, compact, standard, premium, and SUV. Again, number of {v} travelers, as well as their luggage storage requirements, influence choice of vehicle. To rent {v} a car in the UK, one has to contact a rental company. Company agents suggest the best {v} options, depending on your requirements.

answers: A. Tips for selecting a vehicle
questions.title: 20. There are several things to keep in mind if you’re looking to rent a car in the UK. First of all, {v} there are many agencies to choose from in the UK, offering rentals at attractive prices. It is best {v} to compare the prices offered by different agencies before making a choice. The easiest way {v} to do this is to go online and check out their website. Many agencies offer attractive offers {v} and discounts. Online coupons and promotional offers can bring down the costs of rental. {v} Always choose plans that let you do the paperwork formalities beforehand. If paperwork is {v} ready, renting becomes faster and easier.

answers: G. Pay close attention to the legal agreement
questions.title: 21. It is always advised to understand your car rental agreement. While booking your rental car {v} online, it is important to read the agency’s terms and conditions thoroughly. Agencies may have {v} several restrictions, which have to be understood and followed. These restrictions must not {v} be violated at any point in time. One can call up an agent from an agency and ask about the {v} restrictions. Paying extra for additional drivers might be one of these, for example. These {v} additional drivers must also provide valid documents. All this information is kept for legal {v} purposes to ensure protection of vehicles from theft or damage.

22. If you have an auto insurance, there might be no need to purchase CDW and LDW insurance. {v} Pay close attention to the insurance options you’re offered, however. Some only cover {v} damage done to your vehicle by another driver, while not insuring you for anything if you’re at {v} fault in an accident. It’s normally best to play it safe with car rental insurance, since the {v} additional cost is quite low compared to the amount you would have to pay should anything {v} happen to the car while it’s in your possession.

answers: E. Things to know at vehicle pickup time
questions.title: 23. One must become familiar with a car before driving it. You don’t want to find yourself driving {v} down the motorway, uncertain of how to turn on the windshield wipers. When you go to pick up {v} a car, check its functions and parts. It’s important to check beforehand whether there is any {v} damage. Lights, signal indicators, odometers, and wipers must all be checked too. If the car {v} given to you is already damaged, reporting it to the agency is a must. If you fail to do so, the {v} damage will likely be discovered upon return of the vehicle, it will be assumed you caused {v} the damage, and the cost of repair will be added to your costs.

answers: C. Avoid extra charges upon return
questions.title: 24. It is recommended that you return your vehicle with the same amount of gas that was in the tank {v} when you picked it up. Some gas stations offer lower rates. Keep a lookout for these to help {v} reduce costs! It’s common that returning a car late would cause you to be charged extra {v} fees, but pay close attention, because some agencies even charge extra if you’re returning the {v} car before the deadline! So, be safe and make sure to return the rented vehicle on time.

answers: B. Extra tips to follow when returning your vehicle
questions.title: 25. If you are willing to rent a car from the airport, sometimes a shuttle is a more convenient and {v} cheaper option. Those who want to drive on the same day might consider parking away from {v} the airport. This will help to prevent unnecessary surcharges. Always check a vehicle’s interiors {v} before returning it to the rental agency. Many people tend to lose their possessions, as they {v} forget them while returning a vehicle. One must pay attention to the terms, and ask for any {v} deposit returns they are supposed to get back.

exercises.title: 15. Listen to a man and a woman discuss a lecture they attended about the differences between developed and less-developed countries. Read the opinions below and decide whose opinion matches the statements made by the man, the woman or both the man and the woman. {x}

questions.title: Question 1: What is his opinion on nuclear energy? {x}
answers: He thinks that, while nuclear energy can be useful, there are too many negative sides to using it.
He thinks that nuclear energy is generally more reliable and is considerably cheap therefore making it a good option.
He thinks that scientists will discover another type of energy that will be used in the future.

questions.title: Question 2: What is his opinion on the problems nuclear energy can cause?{x}
answers: He believes that the problems that come with nuclear waste are too serious to handle.
He believes that even if humans take safety precautions, the dangers of radiation are too serious to allow the use of nuclear energy.
He believes that the negatives of nuclear energy are all problems that can be solved over time.

questions.title: Question 1: What does he think about homeschooling? {x}
answers: He thinks that homeschooling does not allow students to compete with other classmates.
He thinks that it is more expensive to homeschool a child than to send them to school.
He thinks that more parents should consider homeschooling their children.

questions.title: Question 2: What does he think about sending a child to school? {x}
answers: He thinks that schools allow children to participate more in extracurricular activities.
He thinks that the set curriculum in a school is better than a loose curriculum at home.
He thinks that sending a child to school is more realistic for parents who have to work.

title: Stay Connected
description: Handle real social moments — from recovering a lost item after a party to planning the perfect lunch with friends.
